methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $3.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $3.3B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: TAE Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series G), post-money $6B

data.narrative:
Form Energy, a Series G climate & energy company, is evaluated against a comp universe of 9 private and 1 public frontier-tech peers in the sector, including TAE Technologies, Helion, and Sila Nanotechnologies.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the company's last post-money valuation of $3.5B and applies a typical 1.25x step-up multiplier for Series G advancement, yielding $4.4B. Peer-median analysis benchmarks against three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—TAE Technologies at $6.0B (Series G), Helion at $5.4B (Series F), and Sila Nanotechnologies at $3.3B (late stage)—producing a median of $5.4B and a range of $3.3B to $6.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $3.7B (low) to $6.2B (high), with a median of $4.9B. This range brackets the company's current $3.5B post-money valuation at the lower bound, consistent with stage progression logic. Key uncertainties include the relative maturity of peer companies within the energy-storage and fusion subsectors, the timing and capital intensity of commercialization across the comp set, and the appropriate stage-transition multiplier given Form Energy's capital intensity and extended development timeline.
